domain: Akkadian extispicy (Mesopotamian liver-divination, bārû priest, 2nd millennium BCE): trained priest reads sacrificed liver topology; anatomical regions mapped to canonical omens via clay tablet; multi-priest confer; ritual context conditions validity.
specific_mechanism: EXTISPICY-T-TOPOLOGY-A-ANATOMY-C-CANON-LOOKUP-M-MULTI-PRIEST-R-RITUAL-EVAL: LLM evaluation-diagnostic with (1) T_topology 8-region canonical-organ partition; (2) A_anatomy per-region 4-attribute classifier; (3) C_canon canonical-omen lookup table; (4) M_multi 3-priest concordance aggregation; (5) R_ritual ritual-context conditioning gate.
llm_application: EXTISPICY LLM mechanism + T_topology + A_anatomy + C_canon + M_multi + R_ritual. Differs from R506. R519. R531. R544. R556. R569. Differs from LLM-Rubric + PEARL + Autorubric + RULERS + Judges-Survey by 8-region anatomical-topology + 4-attribute per-region + canonical lookup table + multi-priest + ritual-gate composite.
novelty_claim: Rubric+judge literature covers calibrated multidimensional eval. EXTISPICY adds 8-region T_topology + 4-attr A_anatomy + C_canon table + M_multi + R_ritual. K=5.
